NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO APPROVE PROP 65 SETTLEMENT AND CONSENT JUDGMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF MOORE, JOHN HEARING SET FOR AUG-28-2012 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302
ORDER APPROVING PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENT AND CONSENT JUDGMENT

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JOHN MOORE'S MOTION TO APPROVE PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENT AND CONSENT JUDGMENT (AS TO THE ASPEN BRANDS COMPANY) IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. WHILE THE COURT HAS SOME CONCERNS ABOUT THE REASONABLENESS OF THE FEES AGREED TO BY THE PARTIES AND THE SUFFICIENCY OF THE EVIDENTIARY SHOWING MADE BY PLAINTIFF FOR APPROVAL OF THE FEES, UNLESS AND UNTIL THE COURT HAS DEVELOPED OR APPROVED A BETTER SYSTEM OF EVALUATING THE REASONABLENESS OF FEES AGREED TO BY THE PARTIES IN PROPOSITION 65 CASES WHERE THERE IS NO OPPOSITION MADE TO THOSE FEES, THE SHOWING HERE IS SUFFICIENT FOR APPROVAL OF THE SETTLEMENT. A DECLARATION TO BE FILED BY PROVIDING CONTEMPORARY TIME RECORD REGARDING FEES INCURRED OR SOUGHT IN CONNECTION WITH THIS CASE AND NO OTHER ACTION, AND NONE IS CLAIMED OR WILL BE CLAIMED IN CONNECTION WITH ANY OTHER MOTION FOR COURT APPROVAL. ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED.
RESPONSE TO OSC RE; DISMISSAL FILED BY PLAINTIFF MOORE, JOHN
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE OF OCT-16-2012 IS OFF CALENDAR. JUDGMENT ON FILE. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-10-2012 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-16-2012 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Oct-16-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Prop 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
Off Calendar Aug-28-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. CLIFFORD CHANLER & ATTY. JOSH VOORHEES, TEL. NO. (510) 848-8880 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED REGARDING THE CONCERNS RAISED BY THE COURT ABOUT ATTORNEY'S FEE. PLAINTIFF JOHN MOORE'S Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment (as to The Aspen Brands Company) is Granted. No opposition filed. While the court has some concerns about the reasonableness of the fees agreed to by the parties and the sufficiency of the evidentiary showing made by plaintiff for approval of the fees, unless and until the court has developed or approved a better system of evaluating the reasonableness of fees agreed to by the parties in Proposition 65 cases where there is no opposition made to those fees, the showing here is sufficient for approval of the settlement. A DECLARATION TO BE FILED BY PROVIDING CONTEMPORARY TIME RECORD REGARDING FEES INCURRED OR SOUGHT IN CONNECTION WITH THIS CASE AND NO OTHER ACTION, AND NONE IS CLAIMED OR WILL BE CLAIMED IN CONNECTION WITH ANY OTHER MOTION FOR COURT APPROVAL. ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =(302/HEK)
